sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: Added a tour association tag consistency check to DETAIL pages: the new user-editable TOUR_DEFINITIONS table maps a Springsteen concert tour's official name and date range(s) to a tag (e.g. "Land Of Hope And Dreams" -> tour_lohad), checked via the new checkEventTourTags/findMatchingTours against the event's date. When an event alias is found in the first ("On Stage") tab (extractEventAlias) - or a manual TOUR_NO_OVERRIDES entry - the special tour_no tag is expected instead, for a one-off show (e.g. an award ceremony) slotted into a tour's date range but not really part of it. Routed entirely through the existing computeExpectedTags/isManagedTag/spuriousTagMsg/passingTagMsg machinery (both gained an optional tourCheck parameter), so missing/spurious/passing rendering and the #page-title 'N issues found' tooltip pick it up for free on both the DETAIL page (annotateDetailPageTags) and the YEAR page's Tags button (addTagsButton).

When a real tour event is confirmed (not tour_no), the most specific matching tour's official name (see the new pickMostSpecificTour) is now appended to the DETAIL page's #page-title (new addTourNameSpan/.bb-tour-name), right after the 🏷️ onstage-tags glyph.

The DETAIL page's #page-title now also shows the event alias (extractEventAlias) when present, right after the tour name/🏷️ glyph - the new addEventAliasSpan reuses the exact same .bb-event-alias element the YEAR page already shows, so both pages present it identically; a new #page-title .bb-event-alias { font-size: 0.6em } override (matching .bb-event-type-detail's proportions) keeps it from inheriting the much larger #page-title <h1> font. .bb-tour-name got the same font-size fix.

Added a new 'Show Tour Name on YEAR Page' setting (bbp_show_tour_name_on_year_page, default off, new '🎸 TOUR ASSOCIATION' configSchema section): when enabled, also shows the matching tour's official name on each YEAR page event heading (new .bb-year-tour-name, same italic/bold shape as .bb-event-alias) via the new makeYearTourNameSpan, chained after the existing onstage-tags glyph insertion (new titleTailAnchor tracking in processOneYearEvent) so multiple title decorations insert in the order they're computed.

.bb-year-tour-name now colors the YEAR page's tour name to match the DETAIL page's .bb-tour-name (blue #06c) instead of the event alias's gray, so a tour name reads consistently as a tour name on either page rather than looking like an alias.

Added SONG_COMBINATION_TAG_OVERRIDES, a user-editable, end-user-extensible table for setlist song combinations (medley/tribute entries joined by " - ") that BruceBase tags with a single fixed value for the whole pair instead of one tag per song - e.g. "LAND OF HOPE AND DREAMS - PEOPLE GET READY" -> "lohad.pgr". checkSetlistSongTags now checks each raw (unsplit) setlist song string against this table before its usual per-song split, with a new 'combination' method value threaded through the existing matched/missing tag rendering and tooltips on both the DETAIL page and the YEAR page's Tags button.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: YEAR page: fixed tour-premiere songs losing their bold (<strong>) formatting after processing — renderSetlistElement rebuilds each setlist paragraph's HTML from the YEAR/DETAIL diff rather than reusing the original markup, which previously dropped BruceBase's own bold tour-debut marking. parseYearSetlist now records a per-song `premieres` flag (via the new collectYearBoldSongTexts) alongside each raw/compareKey song, threaded through the diff as `item.yearIsPremiere`, and renderSetlistElement/renderMatchWithConnectives re-wrap the rendered song in <strong> when set.

Fixed collectYearBoldSongTexts detecting zero YEAR-page premieres (causing every song to falsely report a tour-premiere 'Mismatch ❌' against the DETAIL page): unlike the DETAIL page, where a premiere is a -wrapped `/song:` link, the YEAR page's setlist songs are plain text with no link at all — only the premiere ones are individually wrapped in `PLAIN TEXT`. collectYearBoldSongTexts now matches on the `` element's own text instead of requiring a nested anchor.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items:
Added a tour-premiere consistency check between the YEAR and DETAIL page's setlist song comparison: parseDetailSetlist now also records a per-song `premieres` flag (via the new isPremiereEntry, checking each song
  • /

    's existing wrapping), threaded through the YEAR<->DETAIL diff as `item.yearIsPremiere`/`item.detailIsPremiere` on both the YEAR page (processOneYearEvent) and DETAIL page (runDetailProcessing) pipelines, and rendered as `data-year-premiere`/`data-detail-premiere` on every song element on both pages. showSongTooltip's rich tooltip (shared by both pages) now shows a 'Tour premiere' row via the new buildTourPremiereRow whenever either side is a premiere, flagging 'Match ✅' when YEAR and DETAIL agree and 'Mismatch ❌' when they don't.

The `prem` tag is now expected on a DETAIL page whenever at least one tour-premiere song is present (alongside the existing numeric tour-premiere-count tag) — added to MANAGED_CONTENT_TAGS with matching SPURIOUS_TAG_REASONS/PASSING_TAG_REASONS entries, so it's checked/colored the same way as bootleg/storyteller/etc.
Added `'devils & dust': 'dad'` to SONG_TAG_ALIAS_OVERRIDES — neither the exact-match (`devilsdust`) nor the derived alias (`dd`) matched BruceBase's real tag for this song.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: Added a tour-premiere-count tag consistency check to DETAIL pages (computeExpectedTags/annotateDetailPageTags) and the YEAR page's Tags button (addTagsButton): counts the songs BruceBase renders in bold (<strong>) in the Setlist tab — its own convention for a song's tour debut — via the new countTourPremiereSongs/computeTourPremiereTagValue, and expects a matching bare tag value ("1".."9", or "9+" for more than 9). isManagedTag/spuriousTagMsg/passingTagMsg now tell this bare-digit tag apart from the zero-padded day-of-month tag by shape, so e.g. a "6" tag for 6 tour premieres is no longer misreported as a day-of-month mismatch (TOUR_PREMIERE_TAG_VALUES).

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: The 🏷️ 'additional tags found on the onstage companion page' tooltip (makeOnstageTagsGlyphSpan) now groups the tags by first letter with the same .bb-tag-group-label styling used in .page-tags itself (via a new showOnstageTagsTooltip), instead of a plain comma-joined list entirely in warning-red; also dropped the now-redundant 'On Stage' relation-tag rules explanation from the tooltip (removed the now-unused onStageRelationRulesExplanation).

Widened both the DETAIL page's 'N issues found on this page' tooltip (bb-page-title-warn, annotatePageTitleWithWarnings) and the YEAR page's 'N issues found for this event' tooltip (bb-event-title-warn) to 900px max-width to reduce line-wrapping for longer messages.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Venue mismatch/match/extra-text tooltips (YEAR page's renderVenueInfo and DETAIL page's addVenueGlyphDetail) and the 'Info & Setlist' anchor/date/href checks (addAnchorMatchDetail/addAnchorWarnDetail) now render as a .bb-tip-table (same convention as showYearTooltip/showSongTooltip) via new showVenueTooltip/showAnchorCheckTooltip functions, instead of a plain multi-line message — this reliably aligns the compared values (a manual space-padding attempt didn't work, since #bb-tooltip collapses repeated spaces) and lets values render in the tooltip's normal near-white color instead of entirely in warning-red.
Clarified the 'Retail icon' warning's hover tooltip text to say 'in the Recording tab of DETAIL page' instead of just 'in the Recording tab'.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: The DETAIL page's 'Setlist' tab warning (annotateSetlistTab) now correctly detects section label issues too — flagDetailSectionHeaders' warning spans were missing the .bb-section-label-warn class the tab check relies on — and adds a distinct 'Section label mismatch between YEAR and DETAIL page' message when a genuine two-sided label mismatch is present, separate from the generic 'Setlist has differences' message.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Section label mismatches (e.g. 'with Willie Nile' vs 'With Willie Nile') now use a case-sensitive character LCS diff that highlights only the mismatched characters (bold red on light-red background) while leaving matching text unstyled, and the warning icon's tooltip is now a rich tooltip showing the YEAR/DETAIL label variants aligned as rows instead of a plain native title, on both the YEAR page's inline setlist and the DETAIL page's own Setlist tab.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: Add opt-in setting 'Flag Untagged Setlist Songs' (bbp_enable_setlist_tag_warnings, default off) that shows a ⚠️ warning icon/tooltip next to setlist songs lacking a corresponding tag on the event's DETAIL page, both in the DETAIL page's own Setlist tab and inline in the YEAR page's per-event setlist.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: Add 'benefit', 'anniversary', 'interview', and 'funeral' as fuzzy substring-verified tags (renamed ALIAS_SUBSTRING_TAGS to FUZZY_SUBSTRING_TAGS), now checked against both the event alias and a new page free-text notes extraction (checkNotesSubstringTags/extractPageNotesText), wired into DETAIL, VENUE, SONG, and RELATION page tag annotators.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Remove the artificial gap between the page footer and .page-tags on DETAIL/VENUE/RETAIL/SONG/RELATION pages when no tag mismatch is found, by wrapping .page-tags in a new green-bordered '.bb-tags-box' (cancelling BruceBase's own clear:both) instead of leaving it unwrapped; also made tag line-grouping (groupTagsIntoLines) run on all five page types in both the match and mismatch cases, not just DETAIL.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Add song and venue tag overrides: '634-5789 (soulsville, u.s.a.)':'6345789'

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: Added support for the 'nobruce' DETAIL page event type (events Bruce Springsteen did not personally attend, e.g. an awards ceremony he won in absentia — confirmed against nobruce:1986-01-27-shrine-auditorium-los-angeles-ca). Previously the script did nothing at all on these pages: Tampermonkey's own @include pragma didn't match the URL, and even if it did, DETAIL_TYPE_RE gated the internal DETAIL-page dispatch and would have skipped it too. Also added it to KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES (previously the YEAR page showed these events with a '❓ Unknown event type' glyph and skipped the entire name/tag/venue comparison pipeline for them, via processOneYearEvent's isKnown early-return) and MANAGED_CONTENT_TAGS (so the 'nobruce' tag itself gets verified/colorized green like the other event-type tags). Added a 'No Bruce' venue-info prefix on the YEAR page, matching the existing 'nogig' -> 'No gig' precedent. RELATION_TAB_CONFIGS/checkOnStageRelationTags/extractRelations need no changes and remain inapplicable, since 'nobruce' pages structurally have no On Stage/In Studio/On Audio/On Set relation-participant tab (nobody performed).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fixed verified tags/relation names/setlist-tab-label still showing their annotation tooltip ('Verified: ...', etc.) while '⇄ Original Page' view is active — CSS (v3.08) fixed the hyperlink cursor/color, but the native `title` attribute is set directly by this script and isn't something CSS can hide, so it kept showing on hover even though the intent of Original Page view is to look exactly like BruceBase's own unprocessed page (as the DETAIL page's Setlist tab already does, via a pristine listener-free clone with no titles at all). Added toggleAnnotationTitles(), called from both toggle click handlers (setupAnnotationOnlyToggle for VENUE/RETAIL/SONG/RELATION, insertDetailToggle for DETAIL), which moves `title` to a `data-bb-orig-title` attribute on entering Original Page view (for .bb-tag-ok/.bb-relation-name-ok/.bb-setlist-tab-match — the only annotated elements that stay in the live DOM in both toggle states rather than being hidden outright) and restores it on the way back. Verified with a headless-browser round trip on gig:2012-01-14-paramount-theatre-asbury-park-nj.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fixed verified relation names (.bb-relation-name-ok) and verified tags (.bb-tag-ok) losing their hyperlink cursor/color when toggling to '⇄ Original Page' view on DETAIL pages — confirmed with a headless-browser check (gig:2012-01-14-paramount-theatre-asbury-park-nj): the .bb-original-view reset rule used `color/cursor: inherit`, which for these elements (the <a href> itself, not a descendant) pulls from a non-link ancestor (<li>/<ul>) instead of the browser's special link styling, silently swapping the hand cursor for a plain arrow and the link blue for plain text color — while the title tooltip (set directly on the element, unaffected by CSS) stayed visible, making it look unclickable even though the href was always intact. Switched those two rules to `revert` (rolls the property back past all author styles to the browser's native link styling: blue, underlined, pointer cursor) while leaving .bb-setlist-tab-match on `inherit`, which was already correct there (verified: it sits on an <em> nested inside its own already-plain-colored tab <a>, not on the link itself).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Added the 'On Set' tab (recording pages' video-session variant of 'In Studio' — e.g. recording:2012-01-13-mattison-avenue-asbury-park-nj) to RELATION_TAB_CONFIGS (fixedTag: null, like 'On Audio' — confirmed no 'onset'-style fixed tag exists on live pages). Relation names under this tab were already colorized correctly (extractRelations reads tab index 0 regardless of its label), but checkOnStageRelationTags — which drives the green 'verified' colorization on the actual .page-tags tag links — only recognized 'On Stage'/'In Studio'/'On Audio' and returned [] for 'On Set', so those tags never got marked green even though they matched.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fixed relation-name tag derivation dropping accented characters instead of transliterating them, e.g. 'Jörgen Johansson' slugified to 'jrgenjohansson' (the 'ö' silently deleted by the punctuation-stripping regex) instead of 'jorgenjohansson' — causing a false 'missing tag' warning on gig:2012-01-14-paramount-theatre-asbury-park-nj even though the correct 'jorgenjohansson' tag is present (merged in from the /onstage:.../noredirect/true companion page). relationTagSlug() and normalizeRelationTagName() now run stripDiacritics() first, same as songTagSlug() already did; also applied to toLocationTagSlug() (venue/city/state names) for consistency, since the same silent-character-drop bug applied there too.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fixed a residual blank gap now rendering *above* the gold tags-warning box (between it and whatever content precedes it, e.g. a relations list) on DETAIL/VENUE/RETAIL/SONG/RELATION pages — a side effect of v3.04's fix, which moved BruceBase's `.page-tags{clear:both}` clearance from inside the box to just above it, still sized to the floated #side-bar navigation column. Since #main-content is offset via margin-left (not float), nothing actually needs .page-tags to clear the sidebar — BruceBase's own #footer, which follows right after in the DOM and carries the same clear:both, already does that job. Dropped the clear:both from the wrapper entirely (kept clear:none on the moved .page-tags child) across all five wrapper call sites, so the box now renders immediately after the preceding content with no gap at all.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fixed a large blank gap rendering inside the gold tags-warning box on DETAIL/VENUE/RETAIL/SONG/RELATION pages: BruceBase's own `.page-tags{clear:both}` rule clears the floated #side-bar navigation column, and that clearance used to be invisible page background — but once .page-tags becomes a child of our new bordered/background `.bb-tags-warn-box` wrapper, the clearance rendered *inside* the border instead, as a tall empty area above the tag lines. Moved `clear:both` to the wrapper itself (so the clearance stays outside the border, as before) and set `clear:none` on the moved .page-tags child, across all five wrapper call sites (annotateDetailPageTags, annotateVenuePageTags, annotateRetailPageTags, annotateSongPageTags, annotateRelationPageTags).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Add song and venue tag overrides: 'does this bus stop at 82nd street?': 'dtbsa82s' and 'bruce springsteen center for american music':'bscfam'

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Downgraded the 'Event variant on same day' event-title warning (isEarlyLate — YEAR page event name vs. DETAIL page title differing only by a trailing "(Early)"/"(Late)"/"(Afternoon)"/"(Evening)" suffix, e.g. gig:1971-07-29-d-scene-south-amboy-nj-late) from an orange ⚠️ to a new green, informational ⚠︎ (text-presentation warning sign, colorable via CSS unlike the ⚠️ emoji), on both the YEAR page (addYearGlyph) and the DETAIL page's own title (addDetailTitleAnnotation) — new shared makeVariantInfoGlyphSpan() helper. Deliberately not classed bb-glyph, so isYearMismatch's generic .bb-glyph ⚠️/❌/❓ text scan (used by #bb-mismatch-toggle's count/filter) no longer treats it as a mismatch. The .bb-event-title-warn tooltip (collectSectionWarnings) and the DETAIL page's .bb-page-title-warn tooltip (collectPageWarnings) never picked up this glyph's message in the first place (it only ever had a mouseenter-wired rich tooltip, no dataset.msg matching either collector's selector list), so no change was needed there beyond adding bb-variant-info to annotateEventTitleWithWarnings's sibling-walk (so a genuinely different, coexisting issue on the same event still inserts the aggregated ⚠️ after — not before — the variant glyph). showYearTooltip's 'Result' row wording changed from an orange 'Event variant on same day ⚠️' to a green 'Event variant on same day (informational, not a mismatch)'; the now-unused .bb-warn CSS rule (its only use) was removed.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Extended v3.00's venue-mismatch downgrade to a second case: a trailing show-variant suffix — "(Early)"/"(Late)"/"(Afternoon)"/"(Evening)" — on the DETAIL page's venue string that the VENUE page naturally never has (e.g. gig:1971-07-29-d-scene-south-amboy-nj-late's "D'Scene, South Amboy, NJ (Late)" vs. venue:d-scene-south-amboy-nj's own "D'Scene, South Amboy, NJ"). findVenueDetailExtra now checks this suffix case first (strip "(Late)" etc. and compare) before falling back to the existing extra-middle-segment case, reusing the same regex parseEventNameLocation already uses to strip these suffixes before its own tag check. Same downgrade as before: green informational bb-venue-info glyph instead of orange bb-venue-warn, excluded from bb-event-title-warn's tooltip and #bb-mismatch-toggle's count. Tooltip wording generalized from "Venue detail ... not on VENUE page" to "Extra text ... on DETAIL page not present on VENUE page" to cover both cases. No corresponding tag-report suppression was needed for this case: parseEventNameLocation already strips the suffix before deriving location tags, so no spurious tag was ever being deduced/reported for it in the first place.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Venue name mismatch check (YEAR event glyph, DETAIL page venue-link glyph): when a DETAIL page's venue string differs from the actual VENUE page title only by an extra descriptive middle segment (e.g. "Crisler Arena, University Of Michigan, Ann Arbor, MI" vs. the venue page's own "Crisler Arena, Ann Arbor, MI"), this is no longer flagged as an orange ⚠️ mismatch. New findVenueDetailExtra(venueName, detailVenuePart) reuses parseLocationParts's existing venueDetail extraction (the same value the venue-detail tag check already computes) to detect this case; when found, renderVenueInfo/addVenueGlyphDetail render a new informational bb-venue-info glyph (green, using the text-presentation ⚠︎ so it's actually colorable, unlike the ⚠️ emoji) instead of bb-venue-warn/bb-glyph. Deliberately excluded from isYearMismatch, collectSectionWarnings/collectPageWarnings, and #bb-mismatch-toggle counting, so it's purely informational.

The corresponding 'Venue detail' missing-tag report (e.g. the deduced "universityofmichigan" candidate tag) is now suppressed wherever this same extra-segment condition holds: the live DETAIL page's .page-tags annotation (annotateDetailPageTags) and the YEAR page's per-event "Tags" button panel (addTagsButton) — both take a new venueDetailExtra parameter threaded from the venue check (runDetailPage's venue check was hoisted earlier in the function, before annotateDetailPageTags, so the value is available in time; wireIconHandlers gained the same parameter to reach addTagsButton from processOneYearEvent). Applied to both surfaces for consistency even though the request only mentioned the DETAIL page, since they share the identical checkEventNameLocationTags data — leaving one hidden and the other still flagging it would be a visible inconsistency.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Added @connect raw.githubusercontent.com to the userscript header. VZ_MBLibrary's remote changelog fetch (added in v2.96) uses GM_xmlhttpRequest against raw.githubusercontent.com, but that host was never added to @connect, so Tampermonkey refused the request outright ('This domain is not a part of the @connect list') and the ChangeLog GM menu command never appeared.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Added a '⇄ Original Page' toggle button to the HOME page's #bb-btn-container (between ⚡ Issues and 💾 Save), matching the button used on other page types. Clicking it hides #bb-home-results and restores the original #page-title (hidden by runHomePage on load); clicking again reverses it. Unlike the YEAR/DETAIL toggle, this is a plain visibility swap — there's no separate original-vs-processed HTML snapshot on the HOME page, just the aggregated results view vs. whatever wikidot originally rendered there.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Widened the v2.97 sticky-bar toggle shortcut ('Ctrl+M, then B' / bbp_shortcut_toggle_sticky_bar, default Ctrl+B) from a HOME/YEAR-only allowlist to a page-agnostic check: toggleStickyBar() now simply toggles whatever #bb-sticky-bar it finds, so it works on LIST and RECENT CHANGES pages (which also render one) without any further code changes, and will keep working automatically for any future page mode that adopts #bb-sticky-bar. Also added the '⌨️ Shortcuts' help button to runListPage()'s and runRecentChangesPage()'s #bb-btn-container, matching the HOME/YEAR wiring from v2.97.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: Added an Emacs-style keyboard shortcut engine, ported from ShowAllEntityData's ctrlMFunctionMap prefix-key system (sa_ settings renamed to bbp_). New KEYBOARD SHORTCUTS config section: bbp_enable_keyboard_shortcuts (master toggle, default on), bbp_enable_keyboard_shortcut_tooltip (floating hint while in prefix mode, default on), bbp_keyboard_shortcut_prefix (default Ctrl+M), bbp_enable_direct_ctrl_char_shortcuts (default off — direct Ctrl+<letter> combos are suppressed everywhere until enabled, to avoid clashing with the browser/OS), and bbp_shortcut_toggle_sticky_bar (default Ctrl+B). Two dispatch paths share one ctrlMFunctionMap: the prefix key (Ctrl+M) followed by a single character always works; the direct Ctrl+<letter> combo only fires when direct shortcuts are enabled. Wired up the first action: toggling #bb-sticky-bar between displayed/hidden on the HOME and YEAR pages, available both via 'Ctrl+M, then B' (prefix mode) and via the configurable bbp_shortcut_toggle_sticky_bar direct shortcut. Also added: a '?'/'/' triggered '🎹 Keyboard Shortcuts' help dialog, and a new '⌨️ Shortcuts' button in #bb-btn-container on the HOME and YEAR pages that opens it. Dropped from the ShowAllEntityData original as not applicable to BruceBaseParser: the numbered action-button selection (1-9/a-z) in prefix mode (no 'Show all'-style buttons here), the unused isSpecialDialogOpen() guard (dead code in the source too), and createInfoDialog/quick-filter — the help dialog is a plain custom overlay instead.

sections.label: 🔧 Refactor
items: Wired in the shared VZ_MBLibrary (@require file:///V:/home/vzell/git/musicbrainz-userscripts/lib/VZ_MBLibrary.user.js), following the pattern already used by MB_PageEnhancer. Added the new grants GM_info, GM_setValue, GM_getValue and GM_registerMenuCommand. The library is instantiated as Lib with a configSchema (namespaced under the bbp_ prefix, currently a single GENERIC SETTINGS section with bbp_enable_debug_logging), and a remoteConfig pointing at BruceBaseParser_CHANGELOG.json on GitHub raw so the library auto-registers '⚙️ Userscript Settings Manager' and '📜 ChangeLog' Tampermonkey menu commands. log()/logWarn()/logErr() now delegate to Lib.debug/warn/error instead of console.log/warn/error directly — log() output is gated on the new bbp_enable_debug_logging setting (default off), while logWarn()/logErr() remain always-visible, matching prior behaviour. A new fmtArgs()/fmtLogArg() helper joins the variadic log arguments into a single string (Error instances render as their .message; plain objects are JSON-stringified) since Lib's logging methods take a single trailing data value rather than console.log's variadic list.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: checkOnStageRelationTags's "guest" tag check only ever looked for Bruce Springsteen specifically marked "(Guest)" under the "On Stage"/"In Studio"/"On Audio" tab, so a page where a DIFFERENT relation was marked "(Guest)" (a sit-in musician, etc.) never expected the "guest" tag at all. Replaced isRelationMarkedGuest(doc, name) with extractGuestMarkedRelationNames(doc), which returns every guest-marked name (not just one specific person being checked for) — when any exist, "guest" is expected exactly once (not once per name, avoiding duplicate ⚠️guest spans in .page-tags when several people are marked "(Guest)" on the same page), with a label listing all of them. colorizeOnStageRelationNames's per-name-link warning (added in v2.92) was already name-agnostic, so every individually-flagged guest-marked name link was already covered — only the aggregate tag-check/tag-highlight side was Bruce-specific.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Added "featured" tag support, mirroring the existing "help" icon pattern: new hasFeaturedIcon(container)/eventHasFeaturedIcon(eventLinkEl, nextAnchorEl, content) detect BruceBase's gold-star "Featured" icon (), and computeExpectedTags now expects the "featured" tag whenever it's found (either on the YEAR page's per-event block or directly on the DETAIL page itself). Added "featured" to MANAGED_CONTENT_TAGS/SPURIOUS_TAG_REASONS/PASSING_TAG_REASONS so it gets the same green-when-present/red-⚠️-when-missing treatment in .page-tags as every other managed tag, on both the live DETAIL page and the YEAR page's nested "Tags" button.

On YEAR pages specifically, a Featured icon now also gets its own ⚠️ warning glyph (new flagFeaturedIconIfTagMissing, reusing the established .bb-glyph.bb-icon-sorry class/tooltip convention) directly next to the icon itself when the linked DETAIL page's "featured" tag is missing — wired into wireIconHandlers's per-icon loop (title "Featured" isn't in ICON_TITLE_MAP, so it was previously just silently skipped there). No icon-side glyph is added when the tag is present, since the DETAIL page's own .page-tags already shows it green in that case.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Added an entry to RELATION_TAG_ALIAS_OVERRIDES (near the top of the userscript, alongside SONG_TAG_ALIAS_OVERRIDES/VENUE_TAG_ALIAS_OVERRIDES, for easy end-user editing) for relation names whose real BruceBase tag bears no resemblance to the name itself: gig:1993-06-24-brendan-byrne-arena-east-rutherford-nj lists "The 1992–93 World Tour Band" on its "On Stage" tab, but the page's actual tag for it is "otherband" — none of the existing exact/the-stripped/suffix-stripped/nickname-stripped/dotted rules could ever have produced that. Verified the lookup key's en dash ("1992–93", U+2013) matches the live page's exact byte sequence.

Added an Eyewitness tab check to computeExpectedTags, mirroring the existing Storyteller tab check exactly: the "eyewitness" tag is expected whenever the DETAIL page has a non-empty "Eyewitness" tab (not a "Sorry, no ... available" placeholder). Added "eyewitness" to MANAGED_CONTENT_TAGS and its SPURIOUS_TAG_REASONS/PASSING_TAG_REASONS entries alongside "storyteller".

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: On DETAIL pages, each relation name link under the "On Stage"/"In Studio"/"On Audio" tab (e.g. "Clarence Clemons", "The E Street Band") is now itself colorized green (reusing RELATION pages' .bb-relation-name-ok class) when its derived tag passes checkOnStageRelationTags's rules, or gets a ⚠️ warning span with a descriptive tooltip when it doesn't — not just the tag link in .page-tags as before. A name marked "(Guest)" (e.g. Bruce Springsteen) is checked against both its own name tag and the "guest" tag, reporting either or both reasons if either is missing.

Refactored checkOnStageRelationTags's inline "&"-splitting logic into a reusable checkRelationNameTags(name, actualTags, preferDottedTag) — used by both the existing tag-side check and the new name-coloring pass — and factored preferDottedEStreetTag's computation into computePreferDottedEStreetTag(tabMap, relationNames, eventType) so both checks apply the exact same E-Street-dotted-tag rule. extractRelations now also captures each item/member's own <a> element (el) alongside its href/name/extra strings, letting callers act on the live DOM directly instead of only the extracted text.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: On DETAIL pages whose title has an "(Early)"/"(Late)"/"(Afternoon)"/"(Evening)" disambiguation suffix (e.g. gig:1971-01-08-upstage-asbury-park-nj-late's "1971-01-08 Upstage (The), Asbury Park, NJ (Late)"), parseEventNameLocation's state/country tags were never checked: splitting the title on commas left the suffix glued onto the last part ("NJ (Late)"), which parseLocationParts's exact 2-letter state-abbreviation regex (/^([A-Z]{2})$/) doesn't match, so it fell through to treating the whole "NJ (Late)" string as a bogus country name instead of recognizing the "NJ" state — silently skipping the "newjersey(nj)"/"usa" tag checks (they were present on the page but never got the green verified highlight). parseEventNameLocation now strips that trailing suffix before splitting into parts.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: The DETAIL page "⇄ Original Page" button (#bb-global-toggle) was only ever created deep inside runDetailProcessing's setlist happy path (insertDetailToggle, called right after renderDetailSetlist), so it silently never appeared whenever that path wasn't reached — confirmed on all 3 reported pages: interview:2016-09-06-stone-hill-studio-colts-neck-nj (interview pages have no "Setlist" tab at all — getSetlistContainer's #wiki-tab-0-1 assumption resolves to the "Performances" tab instead, which parseDetailSetlist naturally finds no songs in) and gig:1971-01-08-upstage-asbury-park-nj-late / gig:1974-02-24-main-point-bryn-mawr-pa-early (both have a real Setlist tab, but it just says "No set details known." — a genuinely undocumented setlist for these early/obscure shows). insertDetailToggle now runs unconditionally right after runDetailProcessing() completes (previously called only from inside it), and no longer bails out entirely when there's no setlist container — it now always creates the button (matching Save/Load's already-unconditional creation), only skipping the processed/original div-wrapping specific to the setlist itself. Every other DETAIL page annotation (tags, icons, title warnings) still toggles correctly via the existing body.bb-original-view CSS class regardless of whether a setlist exists.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Added ESTREETBAND_DOTTED_TAG_OVERRIDES: 11 E Street Band members (Charles Giordano, Clarence Clemons, Danny Federici, Garry Tallent, Jake Clemons, Max Weinberg, Nils Lofgren, Patti Scialfa, Roy Bittan, Soozie Tyrell, Steven Van Zandt) whose "On Stage" tab tag on a gig/rehearsal page is expected in dotted "first.last" form (e.g. "charles.giordano") instead of the usual concatenated slug ("charlesgiordano") — but only when the tab does NOT explicitly list "The E Street Band" itself. Confirmed against two live pages: gig:1993-06-24-brendan-byrne-arena-east-rutherford-nj (band not mentioned — the "1992-93 World Tour Band" played instead; clarence.clemons/max.weinberg/soozie.tyrell/steven.vanzandt are indeed dotted there) and rehearsal:2026-03-19-youth-temple-ocean-grove-nj (band explicitly listed; the same people's tags are all plain). checkSingleRelationName now takes a preferDottedTag flag (computed once per checkOnStageRelationTags call as preferDottedEStreetTag) that only affects which form is *reported* as the expected tag when neither is present — both forms are always tried for matching regardless, so an exception like gig1993's non-dotted "Roy Bittan"/"Patti Scialfa" still matches correctly. The old unconditional 'jake clemons' -> 'jake.clemons' entry in RELATION_TAG_ALIAS_OVERRIDES (now empty) was folded into this same table, since it's really the same dotted-vs-plain exception, not an arbitrary typo override.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: interview:… DETAIL pages (e.g. interview:2016-09-06-stone-hill-studio-colts-neck-nj) now get the same "On Stage" tab treatment as gig/rehearsal pages. checkOnStageRelationTags's per-relation-name check and "guest" check (Bruce Springsteen marked "(Guest)") were already tab-driven, not event-type-gated, so they already worked for interview pages — confirmed live: interview:2023-10-28-pollak-theatre-west-long-branch-nj marks Bruce "(Guest)" and correctly carries the "guest" tag. fetchOnstageCompanionTags's companion "onstage:" page fetch (for events whose combined tags overflow onto a separate page) is now also attempted for interview pages, not just gig/rehearsal.

One nuance found by comparing several live interview pages: unlike gig/rehearsal, an interview's "On Stage" tab never expects the fixed "onstage" tag itself (checked 4 different interview pages that all have the tab but none has the tag) — extending that specific rule blindly would have produced a false "missing: onstage" warning on every interview page. checkOnStageRelationTags now takes an eventType parameter and suppresses just that one fixedTag expectation when eventType is "interview", leaving every other check (including "guest") unaffected.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: RETAIL pages' "Commercially Released:" line can list more than one release date, one per format (e.g. "April 18, 2026 (Vinyl) / May 29, 2026 (CD)" on retail:live-from-asbury-park) — computeExpectedRetailTags previously only parsed the first date and stopped, silently never checking the rest (confirmed: that page's real tags are missing "may"/"29" entirely). New parseRetailReleaseDates(doc) extracts every date (with its optional "(Label)") via a global regex instead of matching-and-breaking on the first line hit, and annotateRetailPageTags now gives each month/day/year tag's tooltip the specific date(s) (and label) it verifies or is missing, e.g. "Day tag \"29\" expected: matches \"Commercially Released\" date May 29, 2026 (CD) but not present" — unambiguous even when two dates share a tag (e.g. both in 2026).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items:
Added a generic hasUnderConstructionBanner(doc) check (matches BruceBase's `

!! Under Construction !! Come back soon.

` banner by its `` text, not its exact style attribute) and wired the "underconstruction" tag into every page type's own tag-consistency check: RETAIL (computeExpectedRetailTags/isManagedRetailTag), VENUE (computeExpectedVenueTags — now also takes an optional doc param for the YEAR page's nested "Tags" button — /isManagedVenueTag), SONG (computeExpectedYearSongTags/isManagedYearSongTag), RELATION (both computeExpectedRelationTags and the YEAR-page computeExpectedYearRelationTags/isManagedRelationTag), and DETAIL (added to MANAGED_CONTENT_TAGS/SPURIOUS_TAG_REASONS/PASSING_TAG_REASONS and computeExpectedTags, mirroring the existing "help" tag's pattern). Confirmed against the live retail:nebraska-82 and retail:live-from-asbury-park pages, both of which show the banner and already carry the tag.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items:
YEAR page song-number links (``, e.g. "5." before "When You Dance") had no tooltip when rendered by the flat-view setlist renderer (renderSetlistElement, used for

/

-based setlists) — only the list-view (
    /
  1. ) renderer set one. Both renderers' bb-song-num click handlers are wired in the same place (the `a.bb-song-num` click-listener loop after renderSetlistElement builds its HTML), so added `numLink.title = "${songName} — click to load song page tabs"` there too, matching the wording already used by the list-view renderer and by fetchAndToggleSongTabRow's own loading/loaded/failed title updates.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: On RELATION pages, a band/member name listed under the "Bands"/"Members" tab (e.g. "The Rogues", "Bittan, Roy") is now itself colorized green (new .bb-relation-name-ok class) whenever its derived tag (computeExpectedRelationNameTags) is verified present in this page's .page-tags — not just the tag link in .page-tags as before. computeExpectedRelationNameTags now returns {message, links} per tag instead of a bare message string, tracking which source <a> produced each tag (both halves of an "&"-split band name share the same source link) so annotateRelationPageTags can colorize it directly. Hidden/reset under the "Original Page" toggle like every other annotation.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fetching /relation:garry-tallent, /relation:garry-tallent/p/2, /song:the-river, and /song:the-river/p/2 directly showed the server correctly returns page-2 content for every paginated tab on both pages — so v2.83's regression on garry-tallent ("always the data from the first internal paginated page is shown") wasn't a detection bug, it was restorePaginatedTab() redundantly re-clicking a tab that was ALREADY showing: garry-tallent's paginated "Performances" tab happens to be the page's default (tab index 0), so after the "/p/2" reload it's already correctly visible with its own page-2 content — but the unconditional click on its nav link made BruceBase's own tab widget re-fetch/reset that panel back to page 1. restorePaginatedTab() now skips the click when the recorded tab is already the one visible (factored the detection into a shared getVisibleTabLabel() used by both remember and restore). Confirmed /song:the-river's "Performances" tab (index 1, not the default "Credits") still gets switched to correctly, since that tab genuinely isn't showing yet after reload.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: rememberActiveTabForPagination (v2.82) detected the active tab via `.yui-nav li.selected em`/`[title="active"]`, but those markers reflect BruceBase's server-rendered *default* tab (e.g. "Credits" is always tab 0 on a SONG page) and aren't updated when the user switches tabs client-side — so it silently found nothing whenever the user had actually navigated away from the default tab (e.g. into "Performances") before paging, and no tab got restored at all: the page reloaded on the default tab, which — confirmed by directly comparing a fresh fetch of /relation:garry-tallent against /relation:garry-tallent/p/2 — has no relation to the paginated tab's own page-2 content, making it look like pagination was stuck on page 1. Switched detection to the actual, unambiguous signal a tab widget depends on: which `.yui-content` panel has no `display:none` (i.e. is actually visible), not a possibly-stale nav-item class/attribute.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Clicking a `.pager` pagination link (v2.81 fix) is a real same-tab page navigation to e.g. "/relation:garry-tallent/p/2", and BruceBase's own tabview always selects the first tab (e.g. "Credits") on that fresh page load — so paging forward from e.g. the "Performances" tab lost the user's place, landing back on the first tab instead of staying on "Performances". forceNewTab now wires a one-time click listener (rememberActiveTabForPagination) onto `.pager` links that records the currently active tab's label plus the page's path (with "/p/N" stripped) to sessionStorage; restorePaginatedTab(), run once at script start on every page, re-selects that tab (by clicking its nav link, same as a real user would) when the path matches, then clears the stored entry either way.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: forceNewTab (the target="_blank" link guard from v2.71/v2.72) forced a new tab on a tab's internal <div class="pager"> pagination links too (e.g. a "Performances" tab with many results linking to "/relation:garry-tallent/p/2", "/song:the-river/p/2", etc., confirmed via debug/internalPaging.html) since their href still matches the "<category>:" pattern — breaking pagination by opening a new, blank browser tab instead of advancing to the next page in place. forceNewTab now skips any anchor inside .pager via a[href].closest('.pager'), leaving that click to behave normally.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: VENUE, RETAIL, and SONG pages had the same "⇄ Original Page" bugs just fixed for RELATION pages in v2.79 (annotateVenuePageTags/annotateRetailPageTags/annotateSongPageTags all wrap the same outside-#page-content .page-tags the identical way, and all three page types render their own BruceBase yui-navset tabs the same way RELATION does): the tag warning box/highlighting never got hidden, and clicking a tab did nothing whenever the non-live innerHTML clone was the one shown. setupRelationOriginalToggle (v2.79) renamed to setupAnnotationOnlyToggle and reused for all four page types — none of them restructure #page-content beyond simple .page-tags/.yui-nav annotations, so the same CSS-only body.bb-original-view toggle (no clone, no hiding #page-content) applies equally. loadPageCache's toggle-rewiring branch extended to match (venue/retail/song/relation all now use setupAnnotationOnlyToggle after a Load).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: On RELATION pages, the "⇄ Original Page" toggle used the generic setupGlobalToggle: an innerHTML clone of #page-content shown/hidden alongside the live one. This caused two bugs: (1) the .page-tags warning box and green/red tag highlighting live outside #page-content entirely, so toggling never touched them at all; (2) BruceBase's own tab-switching JS (yahooui/tabview-min.js) binds its click handlers to the exact DOM nodes present at page load — the cloned copy has none, so clicking a .yui-nav tab did nothing whenever that non-live clone was the one shown. Added setupRelationOriginalToggle, which instead reuses the DETAIL page's body.bb-original-view CSS-class approach: #page-content is never hidden or cloned (so BruceBase's tab JS, bound once at load, keeps working in both toggle states), and a new .bb-relation-tab-warn class on the empty-tab ⚠️ span lets the existing .bb-original-view CSS rules (already covering .bb-tags-warn-box/.bb-tag-missing/.bb-tag-spurious/.bb-tag-ok) hide every RELATION-page annotation correctly. loadPageCache's relation branch updated to match.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items:
computeExpectedRelationNameTags's Bands/Members tab scan only ever read the tab's first .list-pages-box, but not every relation page follows that layout — e.g. http://brucebase.wikidot.com/relation:bruce-springsteen's "Bands" tab renders its 14 own-bands entries (The Rogues, The Castiles, ..., The Sessions Band) as a bare
    with no .list-pages-box wrapper at all, followed by an unrelated hr/heading/hr, and only THEN a .list-pages-box wrapping a second, separate "Other Bands" list (3 entries) — so every one of those 14 bands was silently skipped, never checked against its tag. Replaced the .list-pages-box restriction with a new collectRelationListLinks(tab) helper: it scans every "/relation:..." link in the whole tab, keeping only ones whose immediate parent element's text exactly matches the link's own text (a clean "
  • Name
    • " entry) — which naturally includes both of Bruce's Bands lists while still excluding a "Members" tab's trailing lineup-timeline prose (e.g. "
  • Clarence Clemons (saxophone)
    • ", where the parent text includes the role annotation and so no longer matches).

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: normalizeRelationTagName (shared by all relation-page name-tag derivations: surname+name, band name, band-part-after-"&"-split, and member name+surname) now also strips "." in addition to whitespace, e.g. "Dr. Zoom" -> "drzoom" rather than "dr.zoom". Combined with the v2.76 "&"-split, a band listed as "Dr. Zoom & The Sonic Boom" on a person's "Bands" tab now expects both "drzoom" and "sonicboom" as tags.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: computeExpectedRelationNameTags's per-band tag derivation (person relation's "Bands" tab, rule (c) from v2.73) now splits a band name containing " & " into its two independent parts and expects a separate tag for each, e.g. "Joe Grushecky & The Houserockers" -> "joegrushecky" AND "houserockers" (two tags, not one combined tag), via new splitAmpersandName()/stripLeadingThe() helpers. Each part still gets the existing trailing ", The" strip as well as a leading "The " strip before deriving its tag. A band name without "&" is unaffected (still yields its single existing tag).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: The YEAR page relation-tab-row's "Tags" button (addRelationTagsButton) only ever rendered "person"/"band" in green, never the surname-letter/Name+Surname/band-member tags added in v2.73/v2.74 for the live RELATION page — those extra checks hadn't been wired into the YEAR page's nested panel at all, so a fetched relation's tags list showed no missing-tag warnings and no green highlight for them. computeExpectedRelationNameTags now takes an optional doc parameter (defaults to the live document; addRelationTagsButton passes the fetched relDoc), and addRelationTagsButton merges its tags into the existing missing/passing computation alongside the managed person/band tags, so the YEAR page panel now matches the live RELATION page's tag-consistency checks exactly.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Extended computeExpectedRelationNameTags (added in v2.73) to also cover band relation pages (ones with a "Members" tab instead of "Bands"): the page tags must include (1) the lowercase first letter of the band name (#page-title with a trailing ", The" stripped, e.g. "E Street Band, The" -> "e"), and (2) one lowercase Name+Surname concatenation tag per member listed on the "Members" tab, each in the same "<Surname>, <Name>" format as a person #page-title (e.g. "Bittan, Roy" -> "roybittan"). Both the person-side and band-side list scans now read only the tab's first .list-pages-box, since the "Members" tab appends an unrelated lineup-timeline as free text after the real list. Reported the same way as the existing checks: green "verified" highlight when present, red missing-tag warning when absent.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: RELATION page tab-emptiness check (annotateEmptyRelationTabs/addRelationTabButtons) judged a tab empty purely from tab.textContent, so a Media tab holding only a YouTube

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Added three more RELATION page tag-consistency checks (computeExpectedRelationNameTags), reported the same way as the existing person/band tag check — including the green "verified" highlight (markPassingTagLinks) when present, not just a red missing-tag warning when absent: for a person relation (one with a "Bands" tab) whose #page-title is "<Surname>, <Name>" (e.g. "Federici, Danny"), the page tags must include (a) the lowercase first letter of Surname ("f"), (b) the lowercase concatenation of Name+Surname with spaces removed ("dannyfederici"), and (c) one tag per band listed on the "Bands" tab, lowercased/space-stripped with any trailing ", The" removed first (e.g. "E Street Band, The" -> "estreetband"). computeExpectedRelationNameTags now returns a Map of tag -> reason so each verified tag gets its own tooltip; this does not add new spurious-tag detection.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Links to BruceBase "<category>:<page>" pages (e.g. /gig:1978-08-21, /venue:..., /song:..., /relation:..., /system:recent-changes) now always open in a new tab, on every processed page (YEAR, LIST, DETAIL, HOME, and the VENUE/RETAIL/SONG/RELATION/RECENT-CHANGES pages). Previously only links generated by SmartTable used target="_blank"; an accidental click on any other category-page link — original site markup, the "Original Page" toggle, Save/Load-restored content, tag/relation links, etc. — would navigate away and lose a page that may have taken a long time to process. A MutationObserver (startNewTabLinkGuard/forceNewTab/isCategoryPageHref) retroactively and continuously applies target="_blank"/rel="noopener noreferrer" to every matching <a href> added anywhere in the document, so no individual call site needed patching. Plain navigation/UI links without a "<category>:" segment (year pages, pagination, Wikidot's own Edit/History/Tags buttons, in-page anchors, etc.) are left untouched to avoid breaking their expected same-tab behaviour.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: The "help" tag check (2.68) only looked for BruceBase's "Help Us" call-to-action icon on the YEAR page, but BruceBase also renders it directly inside a DETAIL page tab (e.g. "Complete lineup of performers is not known" in the "On Stage" tab) — a page with only the DETAIL-page instance got an incorrectly-spurious "help" tag with a misleading "...on the YEAR page" tooltip. hasHelp is now the OR of both checks (hasHelpIcon(section)/hasHelpIcon(document) in addTagsButton, hasHelp-param-or-hasHelpIcon(document) in annotateDetailPageTags). SPURIOUS_TAG_REASONS/PASSING_TAG_REASONS['help'] tooltip text updated to mention both locations.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: computeExpectedTags no longer expects the "memorabilia" tag merely because a tab is labelled "News/Memorabilia" — it now additionally requires at least one in that tab. A tab with only a text "Links" section (news article links, no images) is news-only and was previously flagged with a false "missing memorabilia" warning. SPURIOUS_TAG_REASONS/PASSING_TAG_REASONS['memorabilia'] tooltip text updated to mention the image requirement.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: DETAIL page .page-tags — previously one long unbroken line — is now regrouped into multiple lines by groupTagsIntoLines, one line per group of tags sharing the same lowercase first character (digit-led tags like "17"/"2026" grouped under "#"), each line prefixed with a small bold group-letter label (.bb-tag-group-label). Runs as a final pass in annotateDetailPageTags on both the clean (no-issues) and warn-box paths, so it always applies. As a side effect, tags previously appended at the very end regardless of alphabetical position (missing-tag placeholders, unmatched-song/location/relation candidates) now sort into their correct letter group instead of trailing after everything else. The "⇄ Original Page" toggle collapses the grouping back to BruceBase's original single-line flow via display:contents on .bb-tag-group-line and hides .bb-tag-group-label, so it stays faithful to the original page.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: New "help" managed tag: when BruceBase's "Help Us" call-to-action icon (, the boilerplate "If you have any information ... please get in touch" note shown for events lacking full documentation) appears on the YEAR page for an event, the "help" tag is now expected on that event's DETAIL page. Added via two new helpers: hasHelpIcon(container) for an already-per-event-scoped container (used in addTagsButton via the YEAR page's .bb-section-processed section), and eventHasHelpIcon(eventLinkEl, nextAnchorEl, content) for boundary-scoped scanning of a multi-event container (used in annotateDetailPageTags via the fetched YEAR page content, since the icon is YEAR-page-only and never appears on the DETAIL page itself). computeExpectedTags takes a new optional hasHelp parameter. "help" added to MANAGED_CONTENT_TAGS/SPURIOUS_TAG_REASONS/PASSING_TAG_REASONS for the standard green/red/orange treatment.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: computeExpectedTags now always expects the 2-digit day tag (e.g. "00") even when the event day is unknown ("00"), not just for real days 1-31 — previously the "day is 00" case added no day tag at all. isManagedTag widened to accept "00" as a managed day-of-month tag (parseInt range now 0-31, was 1-31) so it gets the same green/spurious coloring as any other day tag.

New extractEventDaySuffix(hrefOrPath) helper parses the single-letter day-suffix (a/b/c/…) that BruceBase appends to the URL slug's day part to distinguish multiple same-day events, e.g. "b" from "rehearsal:1976-12-00b-telegraph-hill-studio-holmdel-nj". computeExpectedTags takes a new optional daySuffix parameter and, when set, always expects it as its own tag. Wired into addTagsButton (via the YEAR page event link's href) and annotateDetailPageTags (via the live page's own path). isManagedTag widened to accept any single lowercase letter (mirroring isManagedRetailTag's identical existing rule) so day-suffix tags get the same green/spurious/missing treatment as other date tags; spuriousTagMsg/passingTagMsg given a dedicated day-suffix tooltip case.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: ALIAS_SUBSTRING_TAGS (see 2.65's alias-substring tag check) is now keyed by tag -> one-or-more alias substrings, instead of a flat tag list matched against its own name, so a tag can be verified by wording that differs from the tag itself: added "private" (verified by either "private" or "closed" in the event alias, e.g. "Closed Rehearsal"). checkAliasSubstringTags updated accordingly; still fully generic across all event types and tabs, and never flags a missing tag.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: checkOnStageRelationTags now also runs for "nogig" DETAIL pages that have an "On Audio" tab (same relation-name/tag matching rules as "On Stage"/"In Studio"), via RELATION_TAB_CONFIGS['On Audio'] with fixedTag: null (no tag is always-expected just because the tab exists, unlike "onstage"/"studio").

New generic alias-substring tag check (ALIAS_SUBSTRING_TAGS = ['award', 'grammy'], checkAliasSubstringTags): a tag in that list is rendered green with a "verified" tooltip whenever it's both present on a DETAIL page AND a case-insensitive substring of the event alias (extractEventAlias's <strong> header before the relation list, e.g. "grammy"/"award" matched by "68th Annual Grammy Awards Ceremony"). Not tied to any event type or tab; absence is never flagged as missing. Wired into both annotateDetailPageTags (DETAIL page) and addTagsButton (YEAR page's nested Tags button).

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: checkOnStageRelationTags now also runs for "recording" DETAIL pages that have an "In Studio" tab (same relation-name/tag matching rules as the "On Stage" tab), and expects the tag "studio" to always be present whenever that tab exists — analogous to "onstage" always being expected for the "On Stage" tab. Generalized via a new RELATION_TAB_CONFIGS map ('On Stage' -> 'onstage', 'In Studio' -> 'studio') and a relationMethodLabel(method, tabLabel) helper replacing the old ON_STAGE_RELATION_METHOD_LABEL constant.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items:
checkOnStageRelationTags now also expects a "guest" tag when Bruce Springsteen himself is listed under the "On Stage" tab marked "(Guest)" (e.g.
  • Bruce Springsteen (Guest)
    • ), via a new isRelationMarkedGuest(doc, name) helper that reuses extractRelations's existing "extra" annotation extraction.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fixed verified companion-page tags rendering steelblue instead of green: .bb-tag-onstage (steelblue, !important) was declared after .bb-tag-ok (green, !important) in the stylesheet, so with equal specificity it won the cascade regardless of the inline style JS set. Added a .bb-tag-onstage.bb-tag-ok combined-selector rule (higher specificity) to force green while keeping the italic.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: Song tag derivation now strips accents/diacritics before slugifying (stripDiacritics/songTagSlug), e.g. "JOLÉ BLON" -> joleblon, matching computeSongTagAlias's acronym derivation too.

checkSetlistSongTags now splits a song string on " - " (the medley/tribute separator also used by songCompareKey) into independent songs before checking, e.g. "LIGHT OF DAY - HAPPY BIRTHDAY TO YOU" now expects two separate tags, lightofday and happybirthdaytoyou.

checkOnStageRelationTags now handles a relation name containing " & ": first splits into two independent names, each checked via the existing exact/"The "-stripped/suffix-stripped/nickname-stripped/override pipeline (e.g. "Joe Grushecky & The Houserockers" -> joegrushecky + houserockers); only when both halves fail does it fall back to the combined name with " & " removed and a leading "The " stripped (e.g. "Hall & Oates" -> halloates).

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: Extended checkOnStageRelationTags with two more fallback tiers, tried after the existing exact/"The "-stripped checks: suffix-stripped (drops a trailing Jr./Sr./II/III/IV before slugifying, e.g. "Curtis King Jr." -> curtisking) and nickname-stripped (drops a quoted nickname before slugifying, e.g. 'Steve "Muddy" Shews' -> steveshews). Added a new RELATION_TAG_ALIAS_OVERRIDES table (same pattern as SONG_TAG_ALIAS_OVERRIDES/VENUE_TAG_ALIAS_OVERRIDES) for the remaining case where BruceBase's real tag doesn't match any derivation at all, seeded with 'jake clemons': 'jake.clemons'.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: addTagsButton's onstage-companion tag panel entries now keep the bb-tag-onstage class alongside bb-tag-ok when a match is found (previously replaced it entirely), so matched onstage-sourced tags stay italic there too, consistent with the DETAIL page.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fixed a ReferenceError ("Cannot access 'ON_STAGE_RELATION_METHOD_LABEL' before initialization") that crashed annotateDetailPageTags on every gig/rehearsal DETAIL page, aborting the rest of runDetailProcessing (missing 🏷️ glyph, missing gold warning box, setlist rendering/Save button never completing). ON_STAGE_RELATION_METHOD_LABEL was declared as a `const` deep inside the function-declaration section of the file, after the boot dispatch block already `await`s into run*Page(); unlike `function` declarations, `const` bindings aren't hoisted with a value, so the dispatch reached it while still in its temporal dead zone. Moved it to the top-of-file constants block alongside every other shared data constant, which all already followed this convention.

Fixed relation/song/location tags whose only match exists on the companion "onstage:" page never rendering green: the marking loops searched `tagLinks`, a snapshot of .page-tags anchors captured *before* the onstage-companion tags were rendered into the DOM, so a newly-created onstage `<a>` could never be found and colored. annotateDetailPageTags now maintains a `tagToAnchor` map that's extended (not just built once) as onstage tags are added, and addTagsButton's onstage-tag panel entries now check for a song/location/relation match and render green (bb-tag-ok) instead of the plain "found on companion page" style when one exists.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fixed the On Stage relation-tag check (v2.58) incorrectly flagging the always-expected "onstage" tag as spurious (orange ⚠️) at the same time it was marked green/passing: "onstage" is a MANAGED_CONTENT_TAGS member (used on actual /onstage: pages), but computeExpectedTags never adds it for gig/rehearsal pages, so the generic spurious check treated it as present-but-unexpected. annotateDetailPageTags and addTagsButton now compute the On Stage relation results before the spurious/passing pass and exclude any relation-matched tag from spurious detection.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: Added an "On Stage" tab relation-tag consistency check on EVENT DETAIL pages (checkOnStageRelationTags, extractOnStageRelationNames): "onstage" is always expected when the tab exists, and every relation name listed under it (reusing extractRelations, including band members) must have a corresponding tag via exact match (lowercase, punctuation/whitespace stripped, e.g. "Steven Van Zandt" -> stevenvanzandt) or the same with a leading "The " stripped first (e.g. "The E Street Band" -> estreetband). Wired into both annotateDetailPageTags (live DETAIL page) and addTagsButton (YEAR page's nested "Tags" button, .bb-event-tab-row), including missing-tag rendering and green "verified" tooltips explaining which rule matched.

The companion "On Stage" page glyph tooltip (makeOnstageTagsGlyphSpan, used by both the DETAIL-page and YEAR-page 🏷️ icons) now also explains the On Stage relation-tag rules (onStageRelationRulesExplanation), appended after the list of additional tags found.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: Extended the onstage-companion-tags feature to YEAR pages: processOneYearEvent now calls fetchOnstageCompanionTags for each gig/rehearsal event (reusing the DETAIL doc it already fetches) and, when additional tags are found, appends the same 🏷️ glyph (now shared via makeOnstageTagsGlyphSpan) right after the existing match/mismatch bb-glyph on that event's row (addYearGlyph now returns its glyph span so callers can insert further glyphs after it). LIST pages are intentionally out of scope for now since they don't fetch per-event DETAIL pages at all.

addTagsButton (YEAR page's nested per-event "Tags" button) now also merges in onstage-companion tags, rendering them as additional present-tag entries (styled via the new .bb-tag-onstage class) in the sorted panel list, so clicking "Tags" shows ALL tags for the event, not just the ones on the DETAIL page itself.

annotateDetailPageTags now renders the onstage-companion additional tags directly into the DETAIL page's own .page-tags box (as real-looking <a href="/system:page-tags/tag/..."> links, styled via .bb-tag-onstage), then re-sorts the combined tag list alphabetically to match BruceBase's own tag ordering convention, instead of only using them internally for consistency checks.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: Added fetchOnstageCompanionTags: for "gig"/"rehearsal" DETAIL pages with an "On Stage" tab, fetches the companion "onstage:" page (type swapped, "/noredirect/true" appended) and merges its .page-tags into annotateDetailPageTags's tag set, since BruceBase caps tags-per-page and some tags for these events only exist on that separate page. Prevents false-positive missing-tag warnings for tags that genuinely exist, just on the companion page.

Added a new 🏷️ glyph (addOnstageTagsGlyph) right after the DETAIL page title when additional tags were found on the companion page, with a rich tooltip listing them and linking to the companion page's URL.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: The SONG-page exact-title-slug tag check (formerly part of checkSongTitleTagRecognition, optional) is now a hard requirement, split into checkSongExactTitleTag (required, flagged missing if absent) and checkSongAliasTagRecognition (still optional, green-only). Wired into both annotateSongPageTags and addSongTagsButton, including missing-tag-count/button-color contributions.
Added 'tenth avenue freeze-out': '10th' to SONG_TAG_ALIAS_OVERRIDES.

checkVenueNameTag's "At The" venue-name split now passes a descriptive label ("Venue part before/after \"At The\" in ...") instead of the generic "Venue" label, so the resulting tag tooltip explicitly mentions the splitting rule instead of just naming the sub-part.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: Extended the live SONG page's own tag consistency check (annotateSongPageTags) to match the YEAR page's nested "Song Tags" button: computeExpectedSongTags/isManagedSongTag now delegate to computeExpectedYearSongTags/isManagedYearSongTag, so the live SONG page also requires the first-letter-of-title tag (e.g. "BORN TO RUN" -> b) and "lyricsheet" (when the Gallery tab has an image with "lyricsheet" in its filename), not just "song".

Added checkSongTitleTagRecognition: recognizes (without requiring) two more song-page tag conventions — the exact lowercase/punctuation-stripped title ("borntorun") and the derived first-letter-per-word alias ("btr", via computeSongTagAlias). Either is marked green when present, but neither is added to the missing-tag list, since real SONG pages sometimes use only one (or neither, relying on the first-letter tag alone). Wired into both annotateSongPageTags and addSongTagsButton.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: checkSetlistSongTags's exact-match step now strips all punctuation (not just whitespace) before comparing to a tag, so song titles with apostrophes match correctly, e.g. "LIVIN' IN THE FUTURE" -> livininthefuture, "DEVIL'S ARCADE" -> devilsarcade.

Added a venue-name "At The" splitting rule (checkVenueNameTag): a venue name with a descriptive " At The " middle part (e.g. "Blue Cross Arena At The War Memorial") now expects two separate tags, one per side ("bluecrossarena" and "warmemorial"), instead of a single combined-name tag. Applies on both DETAIL and VENUE pages via the shared checkParsedLocationTags.

Added 'rosalita (come out tonight)': 'rosalita' to SONG_TAG_ALIAS_OVERRIDES for the one real exception found so far where the setlist song title's tag is neither the exact-match nor the derived-alias candidate.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Removed "files"/"info" from computeExpectedVenueTags/isManagedVenueTag: they aren't reliably present on every VENUE page, so treating them as always-expected produced false-positive missing-tag warnings.

checkLocationNameTag now handles venue names that repeat their city as a prefix (e.g. venue "Ocean Grove Youth Temple" in city "Ocean Grove"): when the plain full-name slug isn't found, it retries with just the remainder after the city prefix ("youthtemple"), since BruceBase sometimes only tags the venue-specific part once the city itself is already tagged separately. Applied via checkParsedLocationTags passing loc.city as a cityHint to the venue-name check only, so it covers both DETAIL pages (venue named as part of the event name) and VENUE pages.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: The "Tags" button panel caption on the YEAR page's nested .bb-event-tab-row is now the complete raw event name plus " Tags" instead of the generic "Tags"; the .bb-venue-tab-row "Venue Tags" panel caption is likewise the complete venue-page title plus " Tags" instead of "Venue Tags".

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: Added an event-name/venue-name → tag consistency check on DETAIL pages (annotateDetailPageTags, addTagsButton) and VENUE pages (annotateVenuePageTags, addVenueTagsButton): the page title's venue name, venue detail, city, state/province, and country/region are each checked against .page-tags. Venue/city names are slugified (toLocationTagSlug — lowercase, punctuation deleted, no acronym, leading/trailing "The"/"Le"/"De" article dropped); US states and Canadian provinces map to a "<state>(<abbr>)" tag plus "usa"/"canada" (US_STATE_NAMES, CA_PROVINCE_NAMES); other countries map to their own slug plus continent/region extras from a new user-editable COUNTRY_EXTRA_TAGS table (e.g. England -> unitedkingdom, europe). A new VENUE_TAG_ALIAS_OVERRIDES table (empty by default, same pattern as SONG_TAG_ALIAS_OVERRIDES) lets a venue name be mapped to an explicit tag or marked null ("no tag expected") for cases like a generic building name that BruceBase never tags. Matched parts render green with a tooltip; unmatched parts are added to the existing red missing-tag list, showing the expected tag. VENUE pages also now expect two new fixed tags, "files" and "info", alongside the existing "venue"/first-letter-index check.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: Added a setlist-song-to-tag consistency check on DETAIL pages (annotateDetailPageTags) and the YEAR page's nested "Tags" button (addTagsButton): every song in the Setlist tab is now checked against .page-tags via a 3-tier lookup — exact lowercase/whitespace-stripped match, a derived acronym alias (computeSongTagAlias — first letter per word, whole numbers kept in full, dotted initialisms like "U.S.A." keep all their letters), then a user-editable manual override table (SONG_TAG_ALIAS_OVERRIDES, empty by default, placed near the top of the file next to MANAGED_CONTENT_TAGS/PASSING_TAG_REASONS for easy editing). Matched tags render green (reusing markPassingTagLinks) with a tooltip naming the song and match method; unmatched songs are added to the existing red missing-tag list, showing the derived-alias candidate.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Removed duplicate tooltips: one-line messages (spurious/passing tags, para-warn, setlist-tab match/mismatch, empty relation tabs, empty relation-tab buttons, icon-sorry glyphs) previously showed both the native browser title tooltip AND a redundant custom rich tooltip. These now set only `title` — no JS listener, no showOkTooltip (now removed as dead code) or showErrorTooltip call. Genuinely rich/multi-line tooltips (venue match, anchor warnings, song diffs) are unaffected. rewireLoadedPage's generic re-wiring selector changed to `[data-msg]:not([title])` so cache reloads don't reintroduce the duplicate for elements that keep data-msg only for warning aggregation.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Added a relation-specific "Tags" button to .bb-relation-tab-row (addRelationTagsButton), matching the existing Song/Venue Tags buttons: checks "person"/"band" tags against the relation page's own Bands/Members tabs, with the same green (all pass) / red (missing) / dark-orange (spurious) button coloring and ⚠️ warning icon in the label.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Matching setlist songs (.bb-song-match, green) on YEAR and DETAIL pages now show a hover tooltip (YEAR/DETAIL value + green Match ✅ result), matching the existing tooltip support for year-only/detail-only/char-diff songs. Wired in renderSetlistElement (flat view), buildListDiv (list view), rewireLoadedPage (cache reload), and styleDetailLi (DETAIL page).

The DETAIL page's "Setlist" tab label now shows a tooltip in both states: green match (fixed explanatory message) and ⚠️ mismatch (the existing data-msg reason, which was previously stored but never actually wired to a hover listener).

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Tags that pass their consistency check (YEAR event, DETAIL, VENUE, RETAIL, SONG, RELATION pages) are now rendered green with a hover tooltip explaining what was verified, instead of being left unstyled. New .bb-tag-ok class + passingTagMsg()/markPassingTagLinks() helper shared by all annotateXPageTags() functions.
YEAR page Tags/Venue Tags/Song Tags buttons now turn green (instead of staying the default grey) when all tags pass, so consistency status is visible without clicking.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fixed a function-name collision where computeExpectedSongTags()/isManagedSongTag() were declared twice — once for the YEAR page's nested Song Tags button (with songDoc/songTabMap/songName params) and once for the live SONG page's own tag annotation (no params). The later, simpler declaration silently won, so the YEAR page's Song Tags button never actually checked the first-letter or lyricsheet tags — it only ever expected "song". Renamed the YEAR-page versions to computeExpectedYearSongTags()/isManagedYearSongTag() so both checks now run correctly.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: YEAR page event title warning: tag issues (missing/spurious) detected for an event now appear in the ⚠️ tooltip on the event title line. addTagsButton sets data-msg on the Tags button when issues exist; collectSectionWarnings now queries button.bb-event-tab-btn[data-msg] to include them.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: YEAR page flat view: detail-only songs no longer increment the songNum counter in renderSetlistElement. Matches the same fix applied to the list view (v2.44) and DETAIL page (v2.40) — the counter now skips detail-only bullets so subsequent songs are numbered correctly.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: YEAR page list view (☰ List): detail-only songs now show '•' and do not increment the counter, mirroring the flat-view fix (v2.39) and the DETAIL page fix (v2.40). The song href is captured from the bb-song-num anchor before it is stripped, so the bullet remains a clickable link with the same song-tab-row handler.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Renamed '⚡ Mismatches' button to '⚡ Issues' on YEAR, LIST, and HOME pages. Updated tooltips: YEAR/LIST now reads 'Filter to show only events with detected issues (name mismatches, setlist differences, anchor/venue warnings, etc.)'; HOME reads 'Filter to show only events or year sections with detected issues'.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: YEAR page: each event title line now gets a ⚠️ warning glyph (bb-event-title-warn) appended after the bb-glyph when any problems are detected within that event's bb-section-processed container. Hovering shows a rich tooltip listing all issues: anchor mismatches, venue mismatches, section-label warnings, icon-sorry flags, and synthetic messages for setlist diffs (year-only / detail-only / char-diff songs).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: YEAR page: renderVenueInfo was not setting dataset.msg on the venue glyph span, so venue mismatch warnings were invisible to collectSectionWarnings.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: DETAIL page: page-title warning icon (v2.37) now fires when the Setlist tab carries a mismatch warning. annotateSetlistTab was not setting data-msg on the warning span, so collectPageWarnings() (which queries .yui-nav em span[data-msg]) silently missed it.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: YEAR page setlist: detail-only songs now show '•' (bullet) instead of a sequential integer index. The bullet is still a hyperlink with the same click handler.

DETAIL page setlist: year-only <li> entries no longer consume a counter slot in the <ol>. Explicit `value` attributes are now set on all non-year-only <li> elements after insertion, so subsequent songs are numbered correctly regardless of browser CSS counter behaviour.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: YEAR page: added 'Hide Buttons'/'Show Buttons' toggle to bb-section-controls for each event. Clicking hides or shows all tab button rows (.bb-event-tab-row, .bb-venue-tab-row, .bb-song-tab-row, .bb-relation-tab-row) within that event's processed container.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: DETAIL / VENUE / SONG / RELATION pages: a ⚠️ glyph is now appended to #page-title whenever any warning elements are present on the page. Hovering shows a comprehensive rich-HTML tooltip listing all issues (tag mismatches, anchor mismatches, venue mismatches, section-label warnings, empty relation tabs) in document order — making problems at the bottom of long pages visible without scrolling.
addVenueGlyphDetail and annotateEmptyRelationTabs now store data-msg on their warning spans so collectPageWarnings() can retrieve the message uniformly.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: YEAR page: VENUE, SONG, and RELATION tab rows now show a 'Notes' button (first after the label) when the fetched page has free-text content before its first tab navset. Clicking opens a panel with that preamble content (bio, description, address block, etc.).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: RELATION page: removed the native browser tooltip (title attribute) from empty-tab ⚠️ warning spans — the rich HTML tooltip via showErrorTooltip already provides the message, so the native tooltip was redundant.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: YEAR page: added 'Fetch Songs' button to bb-section-controls for each event. Clicking it sequentially loads song page tabs for all unloaded bb-song-num links in that event.

YEAR page: added 'Fetch Relations' button to bb-section-controls for each event. Clicking it sequentially loads relation tab rows for all unloaded bb-rel-bullet participants in that event (deduplicated by href).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items:
Tag checks: 'soundcheck' tag is no longer spuriously flagged when a Soundcheck section is present. The standard DETAIL page format uses a

Soundcheck

header (no colon); the old regex required 'Soundcheck:' which never appears in the raw DETAIL page HTML.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Tag checks: expected day-of-month tag is now zero-padded (e.g. '06' instead of '6') in both computeExpectedTags and computeExpectedRetailTags. isTagPresent already accepts both forms, so actual tags stored without padding continue to be recognised.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Retail icon panel: top-level list-pages-box containers (Wikidot dynamic discography navigators) are now stripped from the fetched retail page content — they rendered as meaningless release icons in the panel context.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Retail icon panel: the retail page title (e.g. 'Magic Tour Highlights [EP]') is now rendered as a bold section heading above the page content, replacing the structureless bb-retail-tabs wrapper div.
Retail icon panel: all tab-label sections (Audio, Video, Artwork, Personnel, etc.) now render with a grey background and start collapsed; clicking a label expands/collapses its content.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: YEAR page: icons whose content cannot be extracted (empty dynamic tab, no extractable links, etc.) are now reliably greyed out (opacity 0.45) by applying the dim unconditionally in the !content branch, rather than depending on sorryTab / list-pages-box heuristics that could silently fall through.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: YEAR page: News icon whose tab contains only an empty dynamic list-pages block is now greyed out (opacity 0.45) instead of showing a warning glyph, signalling it is non-interactive without implying a data error.
Ticket icon: images with 'pass' in their URL path (e.g. files:pass-2008) are now included alongside ticket images when clicking the Ticket icon on the YEAR page.
RELATION page: empty or 'Sorry, no…' tabs in the YUI tab navigation are now annotated with a ⚠️ glyph and hover tooltip directly on the tab label.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: YEAR page: News icon with a dynamic wikidot list-pages block (content loaded via JS) now shows a ⚠️ warning glyph with tooltip instead of a clickable icon that opens an empty panel.

YEAR page: empty or 'Sorry, no…' relation page tabs (e.g. Documents on Bruce Springsteen's relation page) are now rendered as disabled ⚠️-flagged buttons in the bb-relation-tab-row instead of being silently omitted.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items:
YEAR page: for recording events the flat-view relation block is now rendered as
instead of

, giving it the same visual indentation as the recording setlist blockquote. Detection uses sourceEl.tagName==='BLOCKQUOTE' for events with a setlist and /recording: href prefix for no-setlist recording events.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: YEAR page: clicking '☰ List' on a no-setlist event that has relation blocks (e.g. 1965#000065a) did nothing. The list button handler was returning early when setlistEls.length===0, preventing showView('list') from running. Fixed by only returning early when there are no relation blocks either; buildListDiv is skipped but showView('list') now runs, toggling bb-relations-flat/bb-relations-list correctly. Same fix applied in rewireSectionControls.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: YEAR page: events with unknown month/day (date '1965-00-00') crashed with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading localeCompare)'. computeExpectedTags was calling MONTH_NAMES[parseInt('00',10)-1] = MONTH_NAMES[-1] = undefined, which entered the expectedTags Set and propagated to missingTags. Fixed by guarding the month and day additions with moNum>0/ddNum>0 checks.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items:
YEAR page list view: removed the leading
